rtmp/rtmpclient

      • startPacer(client: RtmpClient; initCb: proc (c: RtmpClient) = nil)
        startStreamAacAdtsZeroCopy
      • startStreamAacAdtsZeroCopy(client: RtmpClient; filePath: string; msgStreamId: uint32; csid: uint8 = 4'u8; lowWater: int = 256 * 1024; startTs: uint32 = 0'u32)
        startStreamFlvZeroCopy
      • startStreamFlvZeroCopy(client: RtmpClient; filePath: string; msgStreamId: uint32; lowWater: int = 256 * 1024; startTs: uint32 = 0'u32)
  • Exports

This module implements a RTMP client for publishing live streams to an RTMP server, and a RTMP server for receiving and processing incoming RTMP connections.

It uses libevent for asynchronous network I/O and supports basic RTMP commands, control messages, and zero-copy file streaming. The client can be used to publish live video/audio streams from FLV files, while the server can be extended to handle incoming streams as needed.

Imports

rtmpplaylist

Types

AacStreamState = ref object
  fd*: cint                  ## File descriptor
  pos*: int64                ## Current position in the file
  sampleRate*: int           ## Sampling rate
  channels*: int             ## Number of channels
  ts*: uint32                ## Timestamp
  seqHeaderSent*: bool       ## Whether AAC sequence header has been sent
  msgStreamId*: uint32       ## Message Stream ID
  csid*: uint8               ## Chunk Stream ID
  lowWater*: int             ## Low water mark for backpressure
  done*: bool                ## Whether streaming is done
  tsRem*: int                ## Remaining timestamp for pacing
  preRollFrames*: int        ## Pre-roll frames for AAC
AAC streaming state for audio streaming 
ChunkStreamState = object
ConnStage = enum
  stInit, stHandshakeDone, stConnectSent, stConnectOk, stCreateStreamSent,
  stStreamIdOk, stPublishSent, stPublishing
Connection stages for client state machine 
RtmpClient = ref object
  base*: ptr event_base
  bev*: ptr bufferevent
  msgStreamId*: uint32
  onStreamStart*: StreamFileCallback ## Callbacks for streaming events
  onStreamProgress*: StreamFileCallback ## Callbacks for streaming events
  onStreamEnd*: StreamFileCallback ## Callbacks for streaming events
  onStreamError*: StreamErrorCallback ## Callbacks for streaming events
  onPublishOk*: proc (c: RtmpClient) ## Callback when publish is successful
  aac*: AacStreamState       ## AAC state for audio streaming (if needed)
  ps*: PlaylistState         ## Playlist state for synchronized streaming
  sendTimer*: ptr event      ## libevent timer for pacing
  sendLeadMs*: int = 1200    ## milliseconds of lead to keep ahead of real-time
  wallOriginMs*: int64       ## wall-clock ms corresponding to globalTs==0
  lastSendMs*: int64         ## last time we actually scheduled/sent
  inChunks*: Table[uint32, ChunkStreamState] ## Incoming chunk streams by chunk stream ID
  ackWindow*: uint32         ## Acknowledgement window size
  inBytes*: uint64           ## Total bytes received
  lastAcked*: uint64         ## Last acknowledged byte count
  outChunkSize*: int = 2048  ## Outbound chunk size
RtmpHandshakeState = enum
  hsSendC0C1, hsRecvS0S1S2, hsSendC2, hsDone
Handshake states for client state machine 
StreamAudioCallback = proc (client: RtmpClient; aac: AacStreamState)
Callback for AAC streaming events, called with current AAC stream state (for pacing) 
StreamErrorCallback = proc (client: RtmpClient; st: StreamState; errMsg: cstring)
Callback for streaming errors, called with current stream state and error message 
StreamFileCallback = proc (client: RtmpClient; st: StreamState; bytesSent: int)
Callback for streaming events, called with current stream state and bytes sent in this callback (for pacing) 
StreamState = ref object
  fd*: cint                  ## File descriptor
  totalSize*: int64          ## Total size of the file
  offset*: int64             ## Current offset in the file
  msgType*: uint8            ## RTMP message type (e.g., 0x09 for video, 0x08 for audio)
  csid*: uint8               ## Chunk Stream ID
  msgStreamId*: uint32       ## Message Stream ID
  ts*: uint32                ## Timestamp
  lowWater*: int             ## Low water mark for backpressure
  done*: bool                ## Whether streaming is done
  tsOffset*: uint32          ## Timestamp offset for synchronization
  tagInProgress*: bool       ## Whether we're in the middle of sending a tag (for pacing)
  tagRemaining*: int         ## Remaining bytes in the current tag being sent
  tagPayloadPos*: int64      ## File position of the current tag payload being sent
  tagTsAbs*: uint32          ## Absolute timestamp of the current tag being sent (ts + tsOffset)
  tagSentAny*: bool          ## Whether we've sent any bytes of the current tag (for pacing)
Streaming state for zero-copy file streaming

Procs

proc newRtmpClient(address: string): RtmpClient {....raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create new RTMP client and initiate connection to address. Address should be in form "rtmp://host:port/app/streamKey". Port is optional (default 1935 for rtmp, 443 for rtmps). 
proc onEvent(bev: ptr bufferevent; what: cshort; ctx: pointer) {.cdecl,
    ...raises: [], tags: [TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Libevent event callback for connection events 
proc startPacer(client: RtmpClient; initCb: proc (c: RtmpClient) = nil) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Start pacing loop if not already active, and call initCb for any one-time initialization (e.g. start first stream). 
proc startStreamAacAdtsZeroCopy(client: RtmpClient; filePath: string;
                                msgStreamId: uint32; csid: uint8 = 4'u8;
                                lowWater: int = 256 * 1024;
                                startTs: uint32 = 0'u32) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
proc startStreamFlvZeroCopy(client: RtmpClient; filePath: string;
                            msgStreamId: uint32; lowWater: int = 256 * 1024;
                            startTs: uint32 = 0'u32) {.
    ...raises: [OSError, Exception], tags: [ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}

Exports

PlaylistState, loadPlaylist, nextAudio, nextVideo
